document.title: Guidance for the Submission of 510(k)s for Solid State X-ray Imaging Devices
document.documentType: Guidance for Industry and Food and Drug Administration Staff
authorityOrJurisdiction: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
Center for Devices and Radiological Health
Office of In Vitro Diagnostics and Radiological Health
standards: Radiation Control for Health and Safety Act of 1968
Medical Device Amendments to the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act of 1976

sourceSummary.oneLine: FDA guidance describing information recommended for 510(k) submissions for Solid State X-ray Imaging Devices.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Use to support preparation and review of 510(k) premarket submissions for SSXI devices, including nonclinical data, clinical information when needed, labeling, and quality assurance program content.
keyPoints: The guidance applies to Solid State X-ray Imagers that convert x-ray patterns into electrical signals for medical diagnosis, excluding SSXIs indicated for mammography.
SSXI devices must conform to applicable radiation-emitting product performance standards and may be cleared via 510(k) by showing substantial equivalence to a legally marketed predicate device.
Product Code 90 MQB has been established for SSXIs and they are currently classified in Class II.
Recommended nonclinical information includes physical characteristics, operational functions, functional characteristics, exposure characteristics, safety features, and test results.
Clinical information may not be necessary for certain modifications, but if needed may include either a concurrence study or qualified expert evaluation.

entities.name: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
entities.evidence: Center for Devices and Radiological Health

entities.name: Office of In Vitro Diagnostics and Radiological Health
entities.evidence: Office of In Vitro Diagnostics and Radiological Health (OIR)

entities.name: Division of Radiological Health
entities.evidence: For questions about this document contact the Division of Radiological Health

entities.name: Solid State X-ray Imagers
entities.evidence: This guidance applies to the category of medical imaging devices, Solid State X-ray Imagers (SSXI), that convert x-ray patterns into electrical signals.

entities.name: Product Code 90 MQB
entities.evidence: Product Code “90 MQB” has been established for SSXI’s and they are currently classified in Class II.

concepts.name: 510(k) premarket notification
concepts.evidence: Under MDA, a device may be cleared for marketing via a 510(k) premarket notification.

concepts.name: Substantial equivalence
concepts.evidence: the device must be shown to be substantially equivalent to a legally marketed predicate device.

concepts.name: Predicate device
concepts.evidence: Predicate devices for SSXI’s are preamendment or cleared devices that have the same or similar intended use(s) as the device being submitted for clearance.

concepts.name: Nonclinical considerations for SSXI submissions
concepts.evidence: the following nonclinical information specific to SSXI’s should also be included.

concepts.name: Clinical considerations for SSXI submissions
concepts.evidence: one of the following two options for providing clinical information should also be included if the nonclinical information alone would not be sufficient.

concepts.name: Concurrence study
concepts.evidence: Conduct and report the results of a concurrence study of 30 or more clinical image pairs

concepts.name: Qualified expert evaluation
concepts.evidence: Clinical images from only the subject device may be submitted if they are accompanied by an evaluation of the images by a qualified expert.

concepts.name: SSXI labeling
concepts.evidence: A premarket submission for an SSXI device should contain information on device labeling.

concepts.name: Quality assurance program
concepts.evidence: A submission should contain a complete description of the quality assurance program recommended to the user

evidence.claim: The document was issued on September 1, 2016.
evidence.pageTarget: Guidance for the Submission of 510(k)s for Solid State X-ray Imaging Devices

evidence.claim: The document supersedes the August 6, 1999 guidance with the same title.
evidence.evidenceText: This document supersedes “Guidance for the Submission of 510(k)s for Solid State X-ray Imaging Devices,” issued August 6, 1999.
evidence.pageTarget: Guidance for the Submission of 510(k)s for Solid State X-ray Imaging Devices

evidence.claim: The guidance represents FDA's current thinking on SSXIs and does not bind FDA or the public.
evidence.evidenceText: It represents the Agency’s current thinking on SSXI’s. It does not create or confer any rights for or on any person and does not operate to bind FDA or the public.

evidence.claim: The scope covers SSXI devices that convert x-ray patterns into electrical signals for medical diagnosis.
evidence.evidenceText: This guidance applies to the category of medical imaging devices, Solid State X-ray Imagers (SSXI), that convert x-ray patterns into electrical signals.

evidence.claim: SSXIs indicated for mammography are outside the scope of the guidance.
evidence.evidenceText: SSXI’s indicated for mammography do not fall within the scope of this guidance.

evidence.claim: The purpose is to describe data needed by CDRH to establish substantial equivalence of an SSXI to a predicate or similar device.
evidence.evidenceText: This document is intended to provide guidance on the type of data needed by the Center for Devices and Radiological Health (CDRH) to establish the substantial equivalence of an SSXI

evidence.claim: Before marketing, new x-ray imaging devices must conform to RCHSA and MDA.
evidence.evidenceText: Prior to marketing, new x-ray imaging devices must conform to the Radiation Control for Health and Safety Act of 1968 (RCHSA) and the Medical Device Amendments (MDA) to the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act of 1976.

evidence.claim: Applicable performance standards include 21 CFR 1020.30, 1020.31, and 1020.32.
evidence.evidenceText: they must meet applicable sections of 21CFR1020.30 Diagnostic x-ray systems and their major components, 1020.31 Radiographic equipment, or 1020.32 Fluoroscopic equipment.

evidence.claim: SSXI devices are not exempt from 510(k) requirements because they use a different fundamental scientific technology than film or image intensifiers.
evidence.evidenceText: SSXI’s operate using a different fundamental scientific technology than film or image intensifiers and, therefore, are not exempt from the requirements of 510(k).

evidence.claim: Product Code 90 MQB is established for SSXIs, which are currently Class II.
evidence.evidenceText: Product Code “90 MQB” has been established for SSXI’s and they are currently classified in Class II.

evidence.claim: Nonclinical SSXI information should be included in addition to general 510(k) information required by 21 CFR 807.87.
evidence.evidenceText: In addition to the General Information required for all medical devices submitted for 510(k) clearance (Ref.: 21 CFR 807.87) the following nonclinical information specific to SSXI’s should also be included.

evidence.claim: Nonclinical information may be sufficient for many modifications without additional clinical data.
evidence.evidenceText: Nonclinical information may be sufficient to support the substantial equivalence of a subject to a predicate device for many types of modifications without the need to provide additional clinical data.

evidence.claim: Manufacturers may contact FDA and consider a pre-submission to ask whether clinical images may be needed.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturers may contact FDA and consider a pre-submission to seek feedback on whether clinical images may be necessary based on the results of the bench testing or type of modifications.

evidence.claim: Clinical investigations involving subjects must comply with 21 CFR Parts 50 and 56.
evidence.evidenceText: Investigations involving clinical subjects must comply with 21 CFR Parts 50 and 56.

evidence.claim: If clinical information is needed, one option is a concurrence study of 30 or more clinical image pairs.
evidence.evidenceText: Conduct and report the results of a concurrence study of 30 or more clinical image pairs

evidence.claim: A qualified expert evaluation may use clinical images from only the subject device.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical images from only the subject device may be submitted if they are accompanied by an evaluation of the images by a qualified expert.

evidence.claim: Clinical images should be included in the submission in PDF and DICOM format.
evidence.evidenceText: These clinical images should be included in the submission in pdf and DICOM format.

evidence.claim: SSXI premarket submissions should contain labeling information.
evidence.evidenceText: A premarket submission for an SSXI device should contain information on device labeling.

evidence.claim: SSXI submissions should describe the quality assurance program recommended to users.
evidence.evidenceText: A submission should contain a complete description of the quality assurance program recommended to the user for maintaining the continued proper functioning of the SSXI device

reviewFlags.reason: No product-specific model is identified in the guidance text; productModels is left empty despite productSignal metadata.
No explicit revision or version number is stated; revisionOrVersion uses the stated FDA document number 644.
